channel.title: Posts on Misja Mikkers
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Misja Mikkers

item.title: Academic: the website designer for Hugo
item.description: Academic makes it easy to create a beautiful website for free using Markdown. Customize anything on your site with widgets, themes, and language packs. Follow our easy step by step guide to learn how to build your own free website with Academic. Check out the personal demo or the business demo of what you’ll get in less than 10 minutes. View the documentation Ask a question Request a feature or report a bug Updating?

item.title: Hello R Markdown
item.description: R Markdown This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com. You can embed an R code chunk like this: summary(cars) ## speed dist ## Min. : 4.0 Min. : 2.00 ## 1st Qu.:12.0 1st Qu.: 26.00 ## Median :15.0 Median : 36.00 ## Mean :15.4 Mean : 42.98 ## 3rd Qu.

item.title: Causal inference example
item.description: Introduction In this example we explore “generalized random forests” (grf) to estimate treatment effects in the setting of a randomized experiment. Generalized Random Forests (grf) is a package based on a paper by Susan Athey, Julie Tibshirani, Stefan Wager. grf provides methods to estimate treatment effects. The algorithm is comparable to “normal” random forests, in the sense that it estimates an outcome based on the average estimation in many regression trees.
